delay_description: Existing tenant — tenancy must end before going live

trigger_phrases.phrase: letting agency — August is when notice period ends
friend currently lodging, moving out in ~6 months

typical_timeline.notes: Based on 3 timeline signals observed: unknown, August 2026, ~6 months (Oct/Nov 2026)

how_to_handle_in_meeting.acknowledge_phrase: That makes complete sense — you need to exit that arrangement cleanly first.
how_to_handle_in_meeting.timeline_question: When does the tenancy run to, and have you given or received notice yet?
how_to_handle_in_meeting.reframe_approach: Use the delay period to get everything else ready: agreement signed, photos done, listing prepared — so you go live the day the tenant leaves.
how_to_handle_in_meeting.how_to_keep_engaged: Set a mid-point check-in call halfway through the tenancy period. 'Let me call you in [N] weeks when you're closer to the handover date and we can nail down the timeline.'
how_to_handle_in_meeting.what_to_write_to_monday: Write tenancy end date in date_mm2vmj1q. Set Call Brief: 'Property has existing tenant, expected to vacate [date]. Reactivate [date minus 3 weeks].'

conversion_after_delay.drop_off_reason: Insufficient data to determine — fewer than 10 delay entries per type
conversion_after_delay.note: First run — conversion-after-delay rates will improve with more data over time

do_not_attempt_offer_because: Offering before the tenant vacates creates a commitment the lead can't action. They will either reject it or ignore it — both weaken your position.
